Apologies for the late submission, I had trouble with socoles and assumed it was a global thing that would be fixed so I put it off, but then I realized just now that it works if you use EduVPN. Also, I do not really know how to grade the automated feedback because I didn't use it? I know how to do this question, no need to iterate on my answer.

On the last question I feel like i had the correct answer but socoles said it was wrong, and even if i changed it to what socoles wanted it still said it was wrong. Im not really sure what the answer should be now.
The feedback is too generic and I did not see that there was a submit button, whoops....
It does not recognise spelling mistakes and gets convinced other stuff is wrong when it isn't.

The automated feedback expects one specific answer. When you give another answer that is also right it says its wrong. Also, the autograder does not werk when you write an answer that is not parseable:

"The query is not parseable. Therefore, the semantics could not be verified.
"


At least for me, when I got a 'parseable' query I was already very close to the answer and did not need feedback anymore. I switched to a style where I write parseable queries all the time and then try to use the feedback, but it does not give a lot of hints (except for what your SELECT statement should be. Doing: SELECT * From (any table in the schema) gives you feedback and tells you exactly what your SELECT should be. But that kind of feels like 'cheesing' the system to quickly find out what the SELECT should be before moving on.

In question 4 the RADB query does not seem to match the SQL query.  When running my own query it failed, while it gave the exact same response as the RADB one. I used the automated feedback to make some changes, but it took very long since there was no way to verify if my query was correct.

In some of the questions the phrasing was a bit off? I had a hard time understanding what data to return (e.g., only band member, or also their role, or just everything?).

Thank you for reading my feedback :)

The autograder feels like a chatgpt reskin, which is not what I would want as a student. 
Furthermore, it does not compare the output from the query to the desired output, but it compares the provided query to the model query. 
Lastly, we do not have any experience in SQL, some of these queries are far more difficult than can be expected from us at this time (I am assuming that after week 3, in which we get an SQL lecture we will be more knowledgeable).

I think the answers to questions 4 and 5 are not correct.
Question 4 asks for the natural join, which means you have to use the DISTINCT keyword after the SELECT keyword, otherwise the attribute 'artist_id' will be duplicated.
In question 5, in the WHERE clause, it should be
Track.album_id = Track2.album_id2
instead of 
Track.album_id = Track.album_id, which is a tautology.
It is hard to notice when you typed one letter incorrectly (Making spelling mistakes). For example the last question Graffiti and Grafiti

The space in OverpassGraffiti was not clearly shown, also it does not actually check for the semicolon ";", when grading.
I would also like to point out, that when the program is grading, it always stays stuck on 43% correct, even when you just made a small mistake. 
Also it cannot comprehent spelling errors.
one thing to add in the last exercise we had to compare a duplicate but the answer sad to compare Track.album_id = Track.album_id which should have been Track.album_id = Track2.album_id2
cruddy software

Nested SELECT statements are not supported.
For example, for question 5, the following SQL query would give the exact same result as the "expected" answer, but instead shows a parse error in SOPHOCLES:
```
SELECT track_title
FROM Track, (
  SELECT album_id AS album_id2
  FROM Track
  WHERE track_title = 'Overpass Graffiti'
)
WHERE album_id = album_id2;
```

Often my query has the same result, but will break in socoles.

For example, when checking conditions like A = B = C

you can write either:

A = B and B = C

Or you check:

A=B and A=C

Socoles will break one of them, but not on the other.
